issues.suggested_text_fix: Replace 'DETAILS HERE' with a benefit-driven CTA like 'See How We Work Together' or 'Explore 1:1 Coaching With Kate' to reduce ambiguity and increase click motivation.

issues.element_name: Hero Subtitle - 'This is high-touch 1:1 strategic partnership for established entrepreneurs & leaders'
issues.suggested_text_fix: Replace with a transformation-focused statement: 'Finally step out of the day-to-day and lead the business you actually built — with a strategic partner who sees the full picture.' Make it outcome-driven, not descriptor-driven.
Replace with a stronger hook headline that speaks to the ICA's pain or desire, e.g. 'You've Outgrown Where You Are. Let's Build What Comes Next.' — the current headline is a label, not a hook.

issues.element_name: Body Copy Block - Problem Agitation Paragraph
issues.suggested_text_fix: Strong problem agitation copy — consider adding 1–2 bold callout lines (e.g. bolding 'you're the linchpin that everything depends on') to improve skim-readability and ensure key pain points land for fast-reading visitors.

issues.element_name: 'That's where I come in' Section + Coaching Description
issues.suggested_text_fix: Add specific credentials, years of experience, number of clients coached, or industries served alongside this statement. Vague positioning ('I see the full landscape') needs anchoring with proof: e.g. '10+ years, 100+ business leaders coached across 12 industries.'

The testimonial reads as a written case study (authored by Kate, not Erica). Replace or supplement with a direct quote from Erica in her own words for stronger authenticity. Add a photo of Erica to increase trust signal.

Outcomes listed ('Redesigned her business model', 'Repositioned her in the marketplace') are compelling but lack measurable specifics. Add metrics where possible: e.g. 'Repositioned as a leading expert — resulting in a 40% increase in inbound inquiries within 90 days.'

issues.element_name: Social Proof Section Header - 'Hear from some of my phenomenal clients...'
issues.suggested_text_fix: Replace generic header with a results-anchored statement: 'Real Results From Real Leaders' or 'Here's What Happens When You Stop Being the Bottleneck' — make the section headline do persuasion work, not just introduce.

issues.element_name: Navigation Menu - No visible CTA button in nav
issues.suggested_text_fix: Add a high-contrast CTA button in the navigation (e.g. 'Apply to Work With Kate') so that visitors who scroll back to the top have an immediate, low-friction conversion path without having to hunt for the CTA.

No visible guarantee, refund policy, or risk-reversal language detected on the page. Add a section near the pricing/CTA area with language such as: 'If after our first session you don't feel this is the right fit, I'll refund your investment — no questions asked.' This dramatically reduces purchase hesitation for high-ticket offers.

No FAQ section detected. For a high-ticket 1:1 coaching offer, an FAQ is essential for objection handling. Add questions like: 'How is this different from other coaching?', 'What does a typical month look like?', 'Who is this NOT for?', and 'What results can I realistically expect?'

issues.element_name: Offer Clarity - Next Steps / Process Transparency
issues.suggested_text_fix: There is no visible 'Here's what happens after you click' explanation. Add a simple 3-step process section near the CTA: e.g. '1. Click Apply → 2. We schedule a discovery call → 3. We begin your custom strategy.' Reducing mystery around next steps significantly increases conversion on premium offers.
